structured_conclusion.macro_trend: CPI-Driven Relief Rally, Oil/Hormuz Risk Still Overhanging
structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Semis/Korea beta and cyclicals rebounding; energy still bid on Hormuz risk; duration modestly favored on lower yields
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: FOMC decision Jul 28-29 (14d); Iran-Hormuz risk ongoing
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: Closed 1/3 — USDKRW pass only, EWY and KOSPI still below thresholds despite today's rally

bias: Today's tape is a relief rally: a cooler-than-expected June CPI (core flat vs +0.2% consensus) drove yields lower and lifted Korea/semis/cyclicals sharply off yesterday's Iran-Hormuz oil-shock lows, while equity vol eased. But the relief is partial, not complete — oil is still ~11% higher over the past week and ground up again today, MOVE extended its spike to within 2.5 points of the 80 stress line, and USDJPY remains pinned above the BOJ's 162 line. Structural plumbing (TGA, reserves, net liquidity) was unchanged this run and won't print again until Thursday.

counterargs.thesis: CPI relief supports a near-term risk-on bounce, but oil/Hormuz and bond-vol risk keep the base case fragile
supporting: Core CPI flat (cons. +0.2%), headline -0.4% MoM — largest 1-month drop since Apr 2020
US10Y/US02Y both down on the day, curve easing
EWY +4.77%, SOXX +3.31%, KOSPI +0.73%, DRAM +6.80% — sharp rebound off yesterday's shock lows
VIX -3.85%, USDKRW improved to 1,489.52 — well below the 1,530 BOK line
HY OAS flat at 2.69% — no credit contagion
against: Oil (CL1!) +1.19% to $79.07 — still up ~11% over the past week on Strait of Hormuz risk
MOVE +11.83% to 77.77 — closing in on the 80 stress threshold
USDJPY 162.18 — still above the BOJ's 162 intervention line
Fed Sept hike odds only eased to 63% from 75% despite the CPI beat — market still pricing real hike risk
Korea gate still closed 1/3 — EWY and KOSPI remain well below their SMA20/8,200 thresholds even after today's bounce
counterargs.read: The cool CPI print is a genuine positive surprise and explains most of today's relief — yields down, risk assets up, vol easing. But this looks like a bounce inside an unresolved risk event rather than a new all-clear: oil is still trending higher on the Hormuz situation, MOVE is now flirting with stress-level readings, and the fact that Fed hike odds barely moved despite a soft CPI suggests the market isn't fully buying the disinflation story yet. Treat today's rally as tactical relief, not confirmation that the oil-shock/vol regime from yesterday has resolved.

trade_table.rationale: Sharp bounce off yesterday's Iran/HBM-fear lows (+4.77%/+3.31%/+3.69%/+3.34%), but Korea gate remains closed 1/3 — trend not yet confirmed.
Continuing higher (+1.19% today, +~11% over the week) on Strait of Hormuz escalation — the key threat to the CPI relief narrative.
Yields eased across the curve on the cool CPI print (US10Y -0.93%, US02Y -1.75%); real 2Y proxy fell, not the sticky-front-end pain trade.
MOVE +11.83% to 77.77, nearest to the 80 stress line since this dashboard began tracking — bond-market vol not confirming the equity-vol calm.
HY OAS flat, financials mixed but orderly — no contagion from either the oil shock or the CPI surprise.

catalysts.event: June CPI — printed cooler than expected (core flat, headline -0.4%)
catalysts.note: Consensus was +0.2% core; largest headline drop since Apr 2020. Fed Sept hike odds eased only to 63% from 75%.

catalysts.event: Iran-Israel war / Strait of Hormuz escalation
catalysts.note: Oil still grinding higher (+1.19% today); unresolved — the key overhang on the relief-rally thesis.
KIS research flagged a possible ~8% operating-profit miss on HBM pricing.
No SEP this meeting; cooler CPI complicates the September hike debate.

duration_score.classification: Post-CPI Relief Rally / Broad Yield Decline
duration_score.reason: Cooler June CPI drove nominal yields down across the curve (US10Y -0.93%, US02Y -1.75%) while breakevens ticked only marginally higher and credit stayed calm — a broadly duration-friendly combination. The real 2Y proxy actually fell (1.46% vs 1.52% prior), the opposite of the sticky-front-end pain trade, so today reads as genuine relief rather than a real-rate tightening episode. Caveat: MOVE spiking to 77.8 signals the bond market isn't fully comfortable yet.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: The CPI beat is being read as durable disinflation for now — yields down, breakevens roughly stable, credit unstressed
james_framework.pain_trade: Not currently active — nominal 2Y fell rather than staying sticky, and the real-2Y proxy eased rather than rising
james_framework.watch_signal: Watch oil: if the Hormuz-driven rise continues alongside sticky Fed hike pricing, that combination could revive the real-rate-tightening pain trade even without a hawkish CPI print

t5yie.label: 5Y breakeven
t5yie.note: +0.03 despite the cool CPI print — oil-driven inflation expectations creeping up.

dfii5.label: 5Y TIPS real yield (primary)
dfii5.note: +0.03, the primary market-based real-rate signal — a small tick up worth watching but not yet a trend.

real_yield_2y_proxy.note: Down from 1.521% yesterday — real-rate pressure easing, not the pain-trade direction.

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: Breakevens ticked up marginally (T5YIE/T10YIE both +0.02-0.03) even as realized CPI came in soft — a sign markets are pricing some of the recent oil rise into forward inflation expectations, but the move is still small relative to the scale of the CPI beat.

fx.usdjpy_note: Still above 162 — BOJ intervention threshold, though easing (-0.17%)

sector_rotation.current_regime: CPI-Driven Relief Rally, Oil/Hormuz Overhang Persists

actionable_output.note: Today's price action (yields down, semis/Korea/cyclicals up, dollar weaker) fits a Liquidity Expansion bounce, but it's happening on top of an unresolved oil shock and a MOVE index nearing stress levels — treat the rebound as tactical until oil and bond vol both calm down.
